title: ZooKeeper JVM GC Pause Correlation with Latency
dataSources: GC log lines in `zookeeper:log` (G1/Parallel) joined in time with `zookeeper:mntr` latency
app: Enable GC logging to the same log file or a dedicated file monitored by UF; ensure `-Xlog:gc*` (JDK11+) or legacy GC flags
spl:
index=infrastructure sourcetype="zookeeper:log"
| search "Pause" OR "GC" OR "GarbageCollection"
| rex field=_raw "(?<pause_ms>\d+(\.\d+)?)\s*ms"
| where pause_ms > 200
| bin _time span=1m
| stats max(pause_ms) as gc_pause_ms by host, _time
| join type=inner host _time [
    search index=infrastructure sourcetype="zookeeper:mntr"
    | eval mx=tonumber(zk_max_latency)
    | bin _time span=1m
    | stats max(mx) as zk_max_latency_ms by host, _time
  ]
| where zk_max_latency_ms > 100
description: Correlating long GC pauses with elevated `zk_max_latency` differentiates JVM tuning problems from disk or network faults—critical when sessions drop without obvious external cause.
value: Speeds root-cause analysis and prevents repeated session storms while teams guess at network issues.
implementation: Align clock NTP across hosts. Tune join window (`| join max=0 ... [ | localize]`) if needed; alternatively use `transaction` or `stats` with `strptime`. Increase heap or switch GC algorithm per JVM vendor guidance when pattern repeats.
